channel.title: Jeremiah on Christian Internet Resources
channel.description: Recent content in Jeremiah on Christian Internet Resources

item.title: Jeremiah 1
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 1

1:1The Words of Jeremiah Son of Hilkiah

from the Priests at Anathoth in Benjamin

2 Messages from the LORD came to him from the thirteenth year of Josiah son of Amon, king of Judah. 3 They came as well during the reign of Jehoiakim son of Josiah, king of Judah, till the end of the eleventh year of Zedekiah son of Josiah, king of Judah, till Jerusalem’s exile in the fifth month.

item.title: Jeremiah 2
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 2

Israel Forsakes God

1 A Message from the LORD came to Me:

2 “Go proclaim in Jerusalem that the LORD says,

      ‘I remember your devotion when you were young,

            your love as my betrothed,

            your following me in the open country

                  through uncultivated land.

item.title: Jeremiah 3
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 3

1 God says,

         “If a husband divorces his wife

                  and she marries another man,

                  will he take her back?

                  Wouldn’t that pollute the land?

            But you’re a prostitute with lots of lovers;

                  yet you turn back to me to get help.

item.title: Jeremiah 4
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 4

1 “If you want to come back, you can;

     get rid of your detestable idols

            and don’t waver back and forth with them.

2 Swear, ‘As sure as the LORD lives,’

            honestly, justly, uprightly;

      then the nations will be blessed in him

item.title: Jeremiah 5
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 5

Jerusalem Refuses to Repent

1 “Wander back and forth in the streets in Jerusalem;

         search all around in it.

      Look in its open squares:

      if you can find one honest person,

one that looks for the truth,

            I’ll pardon it.

2 Although they swear, ‘As sure as the LORD lives,’

item.title: Jeremiah 6
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 6

Jerusalem Under Siege

1 “People of Benjamin, get out of Jerusalem!

     Blow a bugle in Tekoa,

            sound the ram’s horn over Beth-haccerem,

            because great destruction

                  looms down from the north.

2 I’ll destroy attractive, delicate Zion.

3 ‘Shepherds’ [commanders] will lead their companies there

item.title: Jeremiah 7
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 7

1 A Message from the LORD to Jeremiah

Jeremiah Speaks at the Temple

2 “Stand in the entrance to the LORD’s Temple and deliver this message, ‘Listen to a message from the LORD, all of you in Judah that go in these gates to worship him! 3 If you mend your ways, I’ll let you live here. 4 Don’t trust in these deceptive words, “This is the LORD’s Temple, the LORD’s Temple, the LORD’s Temple!” 5 If you mend your ways

item.title: Jeremiah 8
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 8

1 The LORD says,

“At that time, they’ll bring out of their graves the bones of Judah’s kings, its officials, priests, prophets, and the people of Jerusalem. 2 They’ll spread them out to the sun, moon, and stars, which they’ve loved, served, and worshiped. They won’t be gathered up or buried. They’ll be like manure on the ground. 3 The remnant of this evil family will chose death rather than life.

item.title: Jeremiah 9
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 9

         9:1Oh that my head was a body of water

                  and my eyes a fountain of tears,

                  so I could weep day and night

                  for my people slain!

            2 I wish I had an inn in the desert,

                  so I could escape from my people;

item.title: Jeremiah 10
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 10

God and Idols

1 Listen to the message the LORD’s giving you, Israel:

         2“Don’t pick up the ways other nations live;

                  don’t let the signs in the sky

                        terrify you that terrify them.

            3 Their customs are delusions.

                  They cut down a tree in the woods,

item.title: Jeremiah 11
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 11

1 A Message from the LORD to Jeremiah

Judah’s Broken Covenant

2 “Listen to this covenant and tell the people in Judah and Jerusalem 3 that the LORD God of Israel says, ‘People are under a curse who ignore the covenant 4 that I commanded your ancestors when I brought them out of Egypt, the iron furnace. I said, “If you listen to what I’m telling you, you’ll be my people, and I’ll be your God.”   5 Doing what I tell you confirms the oath I swore to give you a land flowing with milk and honey, like it is now.’”

item.title: Jeremiah 12
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 12

Jeremiah’s Complaint

         12:1You render justice, LORD,

                        so I’ll argue my case to you.

      Why do the wicked prosper,

            and the devious live in ease?

         2You planted them and they took root;

            they grew and bore fruit.

      You’re near their lips

item.title: Jeremiah 13
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 13

The Ruined Linen Loincloth

1 The LORD told me, “Go buy a linen waistcloth and put it on. Don’t wash it.”

2 So I bought one and put it on.

3 Then he said,

4 “Take it to the Euphrates and hide it in a crevice in a rock.” 5 So I went and hid it there. 6 A long time later the LORD told me to go get it. 7 I went and dug it out of where I’d put it. It was ruined.

item.title: Jeremiah 14
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 14

1 A Message from the LORD to Jeremiah

About the Drought

2 “In Judah’s towns,

         commerce has died off at their gates;

      the people sit on the ground mourning;

      their outcry goes up from Jerusalem.

3 Nobles send servants to draw water,

item.title: Jeremiah 15
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 15

The LORD Will Not Relent

1 Then the LORD told me,

“Even if Moses and Samuel were standing here, my heart wouldn’t be with this nation. Send them away! 2 If they ask you where to go, tell them the LORD says,

                  ‘Those destined for death, go to death!

item.title: Jeremiah 16
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 16

1 A Message from the LORD of Hosts, the God of Israel,

That Came to Me

Day of Disaster

2 “Don’t get married or have children here.” 3 This is what the LORD says about children born here and their parents,

         4“They’ll die from disease,

item.title: Jeremiah 17
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 17

Judah’s Sin

1 “Judah’s sin is recorded with an iron stylus;

     with a diamond point it’s engraved on their heart

            and the horns on their altars.

2 As well as they remember their children,

         they remember their pagan altars and Asherah poles

            beside every green tree on the tall hills.

item.title: Jeremiah 18
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 18

1 A Message from the LORD to Jeremiah

The Potter’s Shop

2 “Go down to the potter’s shop. I’ll give you my message there.”

3 When I got down to his shop, he was there, working at his wheel. 4 But the jar he was making with his hands didn’t come out the way he wanted; so he made another one out of it that he was satisfied with.

item.title: Jeremiah 19
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 19

Jeremiah’s Broken Flask

1 The LORD says,

“Go buy a clay pot. Take some elders and senior priests along 2 to the Valley of (Ben)Hinnom by the entrance to the Potsherd Gate. Deliver my message there, 3 ‘Listen to a message from the LORD, kings of Judah and people of Jerusalem. I’m about to bring such catastrophe here that will make everybody’s ears ring when they hear about it. 4 You’ve abandoned me and made this a foreign place, and burned sacrifices here to gods that you, your ancestors, and the kings of Judah never knew. You’ve filled it with innocent people’s blood 5 and built high places for Baal so you can burn your sons as offerings to him. I never commanded that. Such a thing never even entered my mind! 6 So the time’s coming when this place won’t be called Topheth or The Valley of (Ben)Hinnom, but The Valley of Slaughter. 7 I’ll void the plans that Judah and Jerusalem have. I’ll make them fall by the sword and give their carcasses to buzzards from the sky and scavengers on the ground. 8 This city will be desolate, something to gasp at. Everybody that goes by will marvel at its disaster. 9 They’ll eat their sons and daughters during the siege and distress their enemies bring on them.’

item.title: Jeremiah 20
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 20

Jeremiah and Pashur

1 When Pashhur son of Immer, the priest in charge of the LORD’s Temple, heard Jeremiah prophesying that, 2 he had him beaten and put in stocks at the upper Benjamin Gate of the Temple. 3 The next day, when Pashhur released him from the stocks, Jeremiah told him,

“The LORD doesn’t call you Pashhur, but The Man Surrounded by Terror. 4 He says, ‘I’m going to make you a terror to yourself and your friends. While you watch, they’ll fall by their enemies’ sword. I’ll turn Judah over to the king of Babylon to carry away and kill. 5 I’ll turn over this city’s wealth, its produce, its expensive things, the treasures of Judah’s kings to take to Babylon. 6 Pashhur, you, everybody in your house, and the friends you’ve falsely prophesied for will go into captivity and be buried in Babylon.’”    Jer 20:1-6

item.title: Jeremiah 21
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 21

1 A Message from the LORD to Jeremiah

When King Zedekiah Sent Him Pashhur Son of Malchijah

and Zephaniah the Priest, Son of Maaseiah

Jerusalem to Fall to Nebuchadnezzar

2 “Inquire from the LORD for us; Nebuchadnezzar is attacking us. Maybe the LORD will show us one of his wonderful acts so our enemy will withdraw.”

item.title: Jeremiah 22
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 22

Message About Judah’s Kings

1 The LORD says,

“Go to the king’s palace in Judah and say, 2 ‘Listen to this message from the LORD, king of Judah on David’s throne—you, your officials, and your people that come in these gates. 3 “Be just and good. Deliver from their oppressors those who’ve been robbed. Don’t mistreat foreigners, orphans, or widows, or shed innocent blood here.  4 If you do that, kings who sit on David’s throne will come in these palace gates with attendants riding in chariots and on horses. 5 If you won’t, I swear by myself that this palace will be empty.”’”

item.title: Jeremiah 23
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 23

The Righteous Branch

23:[1] 2This is what the LORD God of Israel says about shepherds that scatter and destroy his sheep instead of tending to them, “I’m about to tend to you for the evil you’ve done. 3 But I’ll gather the remnant of my flock out of the countries where I’ve driven them, and bring them back to their pasture to reproduce and multiply. 4 I’ll raise up shepherds to tend them. They won’t be afraid anymore, and none of them will go missing.

item.title: Jeremiah 24
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 24

Two Baskets of Figs

1 Nebuchadrezzar/Nebuchadnezzar took to Babylon Jeconiah/Jehoiachin son of Jehoiakim, king of Judah, and the officials in Judah along with the craftsmen and artisans. Then the LORD showed me two baskets of figs sitting in front of the Temple. 2 One basket had nice figs in it, like first-ripe ones; the other basket held rotten ones.

item.title: Jeremiah 25
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 25

1 A Message to Jeremiah About the People in Judah

in the Fourth Year of Jehoiakim Son of Josiah, King of Judah

(the First Year of Nebuchadnezzar

King of Babylon)

Seventy Years of Captivity

2 Jeremiah told the people in Judah and Jerusalem:

3 “From the thirteenth year of Josiah son of Amon, king of Judah, till now (23 years), the LORD’s message has been coming to me. I’ve spoken to you again and again, but you’ve ignored me. 4 He has sent you his prophets over and over, 5 ‘Turn from your sinful ways and deeds, and live on the land I’ve given you and your ancestors forever. 6 Don’t worship and serve other gods. Don’t make me angry with what you’ve made with your hands, and I won’t hurt you.’ 7 But you ignored me and made me angry with what you’ve made with your hands to your own hurt.                         Jer 25:1-7

item.title: Jeremiah 26
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 26

1 A Message from the LORD at the Beginning

of the Reign of Jehoiakim Son of Josiah, King of Judah

Jeremiah Escapes Death

2 The LORD says,

“Stand in the court of Yahveh’s Temple and speak to the towns in Judah that have come to worship here. Don’t hold back a word! 3 Maybe they’ll listen so I can change my mind about the catastrophe I’m planning for them. 4 Tell them, ‘If you won’t follow the Law I’ve given you 5 or listen to the prophets I’ve sent you time and again, 6 then I’ll make this Temple like Shiloh and this city accursed to every nation.’”

item.title: Jeremiah 27
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 27

1 A Message to Jeremiah from the LORD

at the Beginning of the Reign of Zedekiah

Son of Josiah, King of Judah

Jeremiah Wears an Ox Yoke

2 “Make yokes and straps and put them on your neck. 3 Send back a message to the kings of Edom, Moab, the Ammonites, Tyre, and Sidon by the couriers that come to Jerusalem to Zedekiah. 4 Have them tell their masters, ‘The LORD of Hosts, the God of Israel, says, 5 “By my power I made the world, its people and animals. I’ll give it to whoever pleases me. 6 I’ve given these countries to Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, my servant. I’ve even given him the wild animals. 7 Every nation will serve him, his son, and his grandson till the time when many nations and great kings make him and his land their servant.         Jer 27:1-7

item.title: Jeremiah 28
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 28

Jeremiah Condemns Hananiah

1 That same year in the fifth month, fourth year of Zedekiah’s reign, Hananiah son of Azzur, a prophet from Gibeon, spoke to me in the LORD’s Temple with the priests and the people standing there:

2 “The LORD of Hosts, the God of Israel, says, ‘I’ve broken the yoke of the king of Babylon. 3 Within two years I’ll bring back the articles from the LORD’s Temple that Nebuchadnezzar took to Babylon.  4 I’ll also bring back Jeconiah, king of Judah, and all the exiles from Judah that went to Babylon. I’ll break the yoke of the king of Babylon.’”

item.title: Jeremiah 29
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 29

1 The Letter Jeremiah Sent from Jerusalem

to the Rest of the Elders, Priests, Prophets, and People

Nebuchadnezzar Had Taken from Jerusalem to Babylon

Jeremiah’s Letter to the Exiles

(2 This was after King Jeconiah/Jehoiachin, the queen mother, the court officials, the other officials of Judah and Jerusalem, the craftsmen and smiths were deported from Jerusalem.) 3 Jeremiah sent the letter by Elasah son of Shaphan and Gemariah son of Hilkiah, ambassadors Zedekiah, king of Judah, sent to Babylon:

item.title: Jeremiah 30
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 30

1 A Message from the LORD to Jeremiah

A Promise to Restore Israel

2 “The LORD God of Israel says, ‘Write in a scroll everything I’ve told you, 3 because the time’s coming when I’ll restore the fortunes of Israel and Judah. I’ll bring my people back to take over the land I gave their ancestors.’”

item.title: Jeremiah 31
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 31

1 “At that time,” the LORD says,

“I’ll be the God of every family in Israel,

       and they’ll be my people.

                  2The people that survived the sword

      found grace in the desert

      when Israel was looking for rest.”

3 The LORD appeared to him from afar and said,

item.title: Jeremiah 32
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 32

1 A Message from the LORD to Jeremiah

in the Tenth Year of Zedekiah, King of Judah

(Eighteenth Year of Nebuchadnezzar)

Jeremiah Buys a Field

2-3 Babylon’s army was laying siege to Jerusalem. Zedekiah had shut Jeremiah up in the courtyard of the guard in the royal palace, and said,

item.title: Jeremiah 33
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 33

1 The LORD’s Second Message to Jeremiah

While Confined in the Courtyard of the Guard

2 This is what the LORD says who made the earth, formed and established it: 3 “Call out to me, and I’ll tell you important things you don’t know.” 4 The God of Israel says this about the houses in this city—including the palace of Judah’s kings—that were torn down to get materials for defending against the siege mounds and the sword, 5 “They’re coming to fight the Babylonians and fill the houses with the corpses of men that in my anger I’ve destined to die. I withdrew because of their sinfulness. 6 Later I’ll bring them health and healing, and show them true prosperity and peace. 7 I’ll restore the fortunes of Judah and Israel and rebuild them like they were before. 8 I’ll cleanse them from their sin against me. 9 This city will have a world-wide reputation for joy, praise, and honor to me. The nations will hear about the good I’m doing for it, and tremble at the peace I’m making for it.”      Jer 33:1-9

item.title: Jeremiah 34
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 34

1 A Message from the LORD to Jeremiah

When Nebuchadnezzar, King of Babylon

and His Army and the Kingdoms He Ruled Were

Fighting Jerusalem and Surrounding Towns

A Warning to Zedekiah

2 Go tell Zedekiah, king of Judah, that the LORD says, “I’m giving this city to the king of Babylon, and he’ll burn it. 3 You’ll be captured and turned over to him. You’ll see him in person, he’ll speak to you directly, and you’ll go to Babylon.” 4 But listen to the LORD, Zedekiah! “You won’t die by the sword; 5 you’ll die in peace. As they burned spices for the kings before you, they’ll burn spices and lament, ‘Ah, lord!’ for you. I’ve spoken.”

item.title: Jeremiah 35
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 35

1 A Message from the LORD to Jeremiah

at the Time of Jehoiakim Son of Josiah, King of Judah

The Obedience of the Racabites

2 “Go bring the Rechabites to a room in the LORD’s Temple and offer them wine.”

3 I took Jaazaniah son of Jeremiah son of Habazziniah, his brothers and sons—the whole family of Rechabites— 4 to the LORD’s Temple to the office for the sons of Hanan son of Igdaliah, the man of God. It was close to the officials’ quarters, above the room for Maaseiah son of Shallum, the doorkeeper. 5 I put pitchers of wine and cups in front of the Rechabites, “Have a drink!”

item.title: Jeremiah 36
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 36

1 A Message from the LORD to Jeremiah

in the Fourth Year of Jehoiakim Son of Josiah, King of Judah

Baruch Reads Aloud the LORD’s Messages

2 “On a scroll, write down everything I’ve told you about Israel, Judah, and the nations from Josiah’s time till now. 3 Maybe Judah will listen to the catastrophe I intend to bring on them to get them to repent. Then I’ll forgive them.”

item.title: Jeremiah 37
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 37

Jeremiah Imprisoned

1 Zedekiah son of Josiah, that Nebuchadnezzar had appointed as king in Judah, ruled in place of Coniah son of Jehoiakim. 2 But he, his attendants, and the people that were left ignored what the LORD said through Jeremiah.

3 Yet King Zedekiah sent Jehucal son of Shelemiah and Zephaniah son of Maaseiah the priest to Jeremiah to say, “Pray to the LORD our God on our behalf.” 4 Jeremiah was still moving freely among the people because they hadn’t put him in prison yet. 5 Meantime, Pharaoh’s army had set out from Egypt. When the Babylonians besieging Jerusalem heard about that, they lifted the siege.

item.title: Jeremiah 38
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 38

Jeremiah Put in a Cistern

1 Shephatiah son of Mattan, Gedaliah son of Pashhur, Jucal son of Shelemiah, and Pashhur son of Malchijah heard what Jeremiah was telling the people:

2 “The LORD says, ‘If you stay in the city, you’ll die by sword, famine, and disease. If you surrender, you’ll have your life as a reward of war. 3 The king of Babylon’s army is going to capture the city.’”

item.title: Jeremiah 39
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 39

The Fall of Jerusalem

1 In the tenth month, ninth year of Zedekiah, king of Judah. Nebuchadnezzar came to Jerusalem and began to lay siege to it. 2 The ninth day, fourth month, eleventh year of Zedekiah, they breached the wall. 3 The officials of the king of Babylon came in and sat down at the Middle Gate: Nergal-sharezer the Samgar, Nebo-sarsekim the Rabsaris, Nergal-sharezer the Rabmag, and the rest. 4 When Zedekiah and his soldiers saw them, they fled at night by way of the king’s garden through the gate between the two walls and headed toward the Arabah. 5 But the Babylonian army caught up with him in the plains of Jericho. They captured him and took him up to Nebuchadnezzar at Riblah in Hamath, and he passed sentence on him. 6 He executed Zedekiah’s sons right in front of him and executed the nobles of Judah. 7 Then he put out Zedekiah’s eyes, bound him in bronze restraints, and took him to Babylon. 8 The Babylonians burned the king’s palace and the people’s houses and tore down the walls around Jerusalem. 9 Nebuchadnezzar exiled to Babylon the rest of the people in the city and the ones that defected to him. 10 Nebuzaradan, captain of the bodyguard, left some people in Judah that didn’t have anything, and gave them vineyards and fields.       Jer 39:1-10

item.title: Jeremiah 40
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 40

1 A Message from the LORD to Jeremiah

After Nebuzaradan Released Him from Ramah,

When He’d Taken Him Bound in Chains

Among the Exiles from Jerusalem and Judah

That Were Being Exiled to Babylon

Jeremiah Freed

2 The captain of the bodyguard took Jeremiah and told him,

“The LORD your God declared this catastrophe on this place. 3 He brought it on and did what he declared. Because your people sinned against him and ignored him, this happened to youpl. 4 But I’m freeing yousg from your chains. If you prefer to come with me to Babylon, come along. I’ll take care of you. But if you prefer not to, don’t. The whole land is open to you. Go wherever seems right. 5 Go to Gedaliah son of Ahikam son of Shaphan that the king of Babylon has appointed over the towns in Judah. Stay with him among the people or go somewhere else.”

item.title: Jeremiah 41
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 41

1 In the seventh month, Ishmael son of Nethaniah son of Elishama from the royal family and a chief officer of the king, along with 10 men, came to Gedaliah at Mizpah. While they were eating there, 2 Ishmael and the 10 men got up and struck down Gedaliah with the sword. 3 He struck down the Jews that were with Gedaliah as well as the Babylonian soldiers there.

item.title: Jeremiah 42
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 42

Jeremiah’s Warning to Stay in Judah

1 Then Johanan, his commanders, Jezaniah son of Hoshaiah, and the people least to greatest came 2 to Jeremiah,

“Please honor our petition. Pray for us to the LORD your God for this remnant. There are just a few of us left, as you can see. 3Pray that the LORD will tell us what we should do.”

item.title: Jeremiah 43
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 43

Jeremiah Taken to Egypt

1 As soon as Jeremiah finished, 2 Azariah son of Hoshaiah, Johanan son of Kareah, and the arrogant men told him, “You’re lying. The LORD didn’t say not to go live in Egypt. 3 Baruch son of Neriah is prompting you to turn us over to the Babylonians so they can execute us or exile us to Babylon.”  4-5 So Johanan and the commanders took to Egypt the entire remnant that had come back from the countries where they’d been driven: 6 men, women, children, the king’s daughters, everyone Nebuzaradan had left with Gedaliah, together with Jeremiah and Baruch. 7 They got as far as Tahpanhes.

item.title: Jeremiah 44
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 44

1 A Message to Jeremiah for the Jews Living in Egypt

in Migdol, Tahpanhes, Memphis, and the Territory of Pathros

Disaster Because of Idolatry

2 “Yahveh of Hosts, the God of Israel, says, ‘You’ve seen the catastrophe I brought on Jerusalem and the towns in Judah; they’re in ruins. Nobody’s living in them 3 because their sinfulness made me angry.  They kept serving and burning sacrifices to gods they and their ancestors hadn’t known. 4 Yet I sent them my prophets time and again, saying not to do that detestable thing. 5 ‘But they didn’t listen, turn from their sin, or quit burning sacrifices to other gods. 6 So my anger boiled over and burned in the towns of Judah and the streets of Jerusalem.’ 7 Now Yahveh says, ‘Why harm yourselves and slay man and woman, child and infant, from Judah and leave yourselves without even a remnant? 8 You’ve made me angry with the things you’ve made with your hands and the sacrifices you’ve burned to other gods in Egypt. You’ll be destroyed and become a curse and disgrace among the nations. 9 Have you forgotten your ancestors’ sinfulness, the sinfulness of Judah’s kings and their wives, the sinfulness you and your wives committed in Judah and the streets of Jerusalem? 10 You haven’t humbled yourselves or respected me to this day or followed the Law and statutes I set before you and your ancestors.’

item.title: Jeremiah 45
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 45

1 The Message Jeremiah Spoke to Baruch

When He’d Written It in a Scroll at Jeremiah’s Dictation

in the Fourth Year of Jehoiakim Son of Josiah, King of Judah

A Message for Baruch

2 “This is what the LORD God of Israel says to you, Baruch: 3 ‘You said, “Misfortune awaits me! The LORD has added sorrow to pain. I’m exhausted from groaning. I can’t sleep.”’ 4 Tell him the LORD says, ‘What I’ve built up I’m about to tear down. What I’ve planted I’m about to uproot—the whole land. 5 Are you anticipating great things for yourself? Don’t. I’m going to bring disaster on everybody, but I’ll give you your life as a reward of war wherever you go.’”

item.title: Jeremiah 46
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 46

1 A Message from the LORD to Jeremiah About the Nations

2 A Message to Egypt about Pharaoh Necho’s Army

That Was by the Euphrates River at Carchemish,

Which Nebuchadnezzar Defeated

in the Fourth Year of Jehoiakim Son of Josiah, King of Judah

item.title: Jeremiah 47
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 47

1 A Message from the LORD to Jeremiah

About the Philistines Before Pharaoh Conquered Gaza

2 The LORD says,

            “A flood is swelling up from the north;

                  it will become a tsunami.

            It’ll flood the land and everything in it,

item.title: Jeremiah 48
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 48

1 About Moab

The LORD of Hosts, the God of Israel, says,

            “Misfortune awaits Nebo—laid waste;

                  Kiriathaim will be disgraced—taken;     

                  the fortress will be humiliated—torn down.

                  2No more renown for Moab;

                  disaster’s in store for Heshbon

                        to cease being a nation!

item.title: Jeremiah 49
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 49

1 About Ammon

The LORD says,

      “Doesn’t Israel have any descendants?

            Doesn’t he have an heir?

      Why then has Malcam/Milcom taken possession of Gad,

            and his people settled in its towns?

         2The time’s coming

            when I’ll raise the battle cry

item.title: Jeremiah 50
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 50

1 A Message the LORD Spoke About Babylon,

the Land of the Babylonians, Through the Prophet Jeremiah:

2 “Announce it among the nations;

         hoist a standard everyone can see:

                        ‘Babylon has been captured,

                         Bel humbled,

                        Marduk shattered,

item.title: Jeremiah 51
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 51

1 The LORD says,

      “I’m going to stir up the spirit of the destroyer

            against Babylon and the people in Leb-kamai.

2 I’ll send foreign winnowers to Babylon

            to devastate its land.

On every side they’ll oppose it

            at the time of its catastrophe.

item.title: Jeremiah 52
item.description:
Jeremiah Chapter 52

The Fall of Jerusalem

1 Zedekiah was 21 when he became king and ruled for 11 years in Jerusalem. His mother was Hamutal, daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah. 2 He did all the evil Jehoiakim had done. 3 Because of the LORD’s anger over that, he put Jerusalem and Judah out of his presence.

Zedekiah revolted against the king of Babylon. 4 The tenth day, tenth month, ninth year of Zedekiah’s reign, Nebuchadnezzar came with his army, set up camp against Jerusalem, and built a siege wall around it. 5 The city lay under siege till Zedekiah’s eleventh year. 6 The ninth day, fourth month, the famine was so severe in the city that the people didn’t have any food left. 7 Then they broke into the city, and the fighting men fled at night through the gate between the two walls by the king’s garden, even though the Babylonians were all around the city. They went toward the Arabah, 8 but the Babylonian army overtook the king in the plains by Jericho. His whole army scattered from him. 9 They captured him and took him up to the king of Babylon at Riblah in Hamath, and he passed sentence on him. 10 He executed Zedekiah’s sons right in front of him and executed the officials of Judah. 11 Then he put out Zedekiah’s eyes, bound him with bronze bonds, and took him to Babylon. He put him in prison till his death.
